sourceFiles.name: .fixed-models/thermal-system__review-2026-03-26T09-10-14-037Z__selected-1-issues__2026-03-26T09-11-07-061Z.sysml

results.name: 04_redundancy
results.rawText:
System characterisation:
- System purpose: A thermal control/protection fragment defining shutdown and cooling activation thresholds for a cooling system.
- Operational domain: Industrial/mechanical thermal management (domain-specific context not further specified).
- Model scope: Small model fragment containing two requirements and one part definition with threshold attributes.
- Known scope limitations: No behaviour model, no state machines, no packages beyond the root package, no verification cases, no relationships (`satisfy`, `allocate`, `refine`, etc.), and no decomposition hierarchy. Checks depending on those constructs are assessed as not evidenced in this fragment and passed/skipped accordingly.

CHECK PASSED: 1. Duplicate Requirements  -  The two requirement definitions have distinct texts and state different trigger temperatures (80°C shutdown vs 85°C fan activation), so no direct textual duplication of requirements is present.

CHECK PASSED: 2. Duplicate Block or Part Definitions  -  The fragment contains only one `part def` (`CoolingSystem`) and no duplicate `item def`, alternate component definitions, or repeated interface types.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Redundant Relationships  -  No explicit `satisfy`, `allocate`, `connect`, `bind`, or specialization relationships are present, so no duplicated relationships were found in this fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 4. Redundant Behaviour Models  -  No state machines, action definitions, sequences, or message exchange models are present, so there is no behavioural duplication to assess.

CHECK PASSED: 5. Redundant Attributes & Constraints  -  The three attributes on `CoolingSystem` are distinct values with different semantic roles (`temperatureC`, `emergencyShutdownThresholdC`, `coolingFanStartThresholdC`); no repeated attribute definitions, copied derived values, or redundant unit restatements were found.

CHECK PASSED: 6. Redundant Decomposition  -  The model has no decomposition hierarchy, intermediate pass-through blocks, empty packages, or action decomposition layers, so no redundant decomposition is present.

CHECK PASSED: 7. Unintentional vs. Intentional Redundancy  -  No duplicated structural or requirements content was identified, so there is no redundancy needing intentionality annotation.

ISSUE [REDUNDANCY-001]
Element(s):     thermal_system::EmergencyShutdown; thermal_system::CoolingFanActivation; thermal_system::CoolingSystem
Check:          8. States that are never activated
Finding:        The requirement `CoolingFanActivation` specifies fan activation at 85°C, while `EmergencyShutdown` specifies system shutdown at 80°C. In the same model fragment, `CoolingSystem` also defines `emergencyShutdownThresholdC = 80.0` and `coolingFanStartThresholdC = 75.0`, creating an unresolved duplicate threshold definition for fan activation. If the requirements are taken as authoritative, the fan-activation condition at 85°C is unreachable because shutdown occurs first at 80°C, making that modelled activation logic redundant.
Evidence:       `requirement def EmergencyShutdown { doc /* The system shall enter emergency shutdown when temperature reaches 80 degrees Celsius. */ }`; `requirement def CoolingFanActivation { doc /* The cooling fan shall turn on when temperature reaches 85 degrees Celsius. */ }`; `part def CoolingSystem { attribute emergencyShutdownThresholdC : Real = 80.0; attribute coolingFanStartThresholdC : Real = 75.0; }`
Score:          0.4  -  Significant defect because conflicting duplicated threshold intent can make one modelled condition unreachable and mislead analysis of thermal response.
Confidence:     HIGH  -  The temperature values and their conflict are explicit in the model text.
Risk:           Analysts or implementers may choose different threshold sources (85°C requirement vs 75°C attribute), causing divergence between requirements and design. If the 85°C requirement is followed, the fan activation state is unreachable due to shutdown at 80°C; if the 75°C attribute is followed, the requirement is violated. This creates inconsistency and undermines traceable thermal-control behaviour.
Recommendation: Remove the duplicate threshold definition or align the sources using a single authoritative definition. Either (a) change `CoolingFanActivation` to match the design threshold, (b) change `coolingFanStartThresholdC` to match the requirement, or (c) explicitly derive one from the other with traceability. Also clarify behavioural precedence so the fan activation condition is not modelled as reachable if shutdown precludes it.

Summary Table

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| REDUNDANCY-001 | 0.4 | HIGH | Fan activation threshold intent is duplicated inconsistently (85°C requirement vs 75°C attribute), and the 85°C condition is unreachable after 80°C shutdown. |

Overall Assessment Score: 0.4
Model Status: Critical deficiencies - major rework required before this model can be used
